theme: H1 is over — July is where winners adjust, not January

weeks.theme: PROBLEM — Agitate the pain of a closed H1 with no clear data

posts.hook: H1 just ended. Do you actually know what your sales team did?
posts.body:
H1 just ended. Do you actually know what your sales team did?

Not what they said they did.
Not the WhatsApp voice notes.
Not the number Kofi mentioned in the Monday meeting.

Actual calls. Actual leads contacted. Actual follow-ups that happened.

Most business owners I talk to can't answer that question honestly.
They have a feeling. A rough number. A rep who "seems busy."

July is the reset month. H2 starts now.

But you can't adjust what you never measured.

What does your H1 sales data actually look like? Drop a comment — honest answers only. 👇
posts.cta: Drop a comment — honest answers only.
posts.graphic_brief: Bold text on dark background: 'H1 is done. Do you know what your team actually did?' — leads2conv logo bottom right

posts.body: H1 is done. 🗓️ Can you prove what your reps did? Most managers can't.
posts.graphic_brief: Split graphic: left side calendar showing June crossed out, right side question mark. leads2conv branding.

posts.hook: Your rep said he made 40 calls yesterday. 🤔
posts.body:
Your rep said he made 40 calls yesterday. 🤔

But his pipeline has 2 new leads.
His follow-up list hasn't moved.
And you have zero proof.

This is Ghana sales reality for most SMEs.

You're not managing a sales team — you're managing stories.

H2 starts now. Is July going to be different, or the same?

Tell us in the comments 👇
posts.graphic_brief: Cartoon rep on phone looking relaxed while boss checks empty spreadsheet. Light, relatable tone.

posts.body: "40 calls today boss" 📱 No log. No proof. No leads. Sound familiar?

posts.hook: The rep resigned on Friday. By Monday, the leads were gone.
posts.body:
The rep resigned on Friday. By Monday, the leads were gone.

Not the company's leads.
His leads. In his phone. In his WhatsApp. In his head.

The prospects he had been nurturing for three months?
They're getting calls from his new employer now.

This isn't a loyalty problem. It's a systems problem.

When leads live in reps' phones, they leave with the rep.
Every. Single. Time.

How many leads has your business lost this way? 
Be honest. 👇
posts.cta: How many leads has your business lost this way?
posts.graphic_brief: Visual of a phone walking out a door carrying a briefcase labeled 'YOUR LEADS'. Bold and simple.

posts.body: Rep leaves Friday. Your leads leave with him. 🚪 That's not loyalty — it's a systems problem.

posts.hook: Saturday check-in for sales managers in Ghana 👋
posts.body:
Saturday check-in for sales managers in Ghana 👋

Quick question: when your team submits their weekly report on Friday...

Is it a real record of what happened?
Or is it a creative writing exercise done from memory at 4:45pm?

😅 We know how it goes.

H2 starts Monday. This week on leads2conv, we'll be talking about how to actually fix this.

Have a good weekend — rest well so you can run hard on Monday! 💪

weeks.theme: PRODUCT — One feature per post, framed as a benefit scene

posts.hook: 8:00am Monday. You open leads2conv and you already know.
posts.body:
8:00am Monday. You open leads2conv and you already know.

Who called who. How many times. What was said. What happens next.

No waiting for the team meeting.
No chasing reps for updates.
No "I think we spoke to them last month."

leads2conv gives you a live daily dashboard — every rep's activity, right there.

Calls logged. Leads tracked. Follow-ups scheduled.

You see it in real time. Not at month-end.
Not when it's too late to correct course.

That's what it means to actually manage a sales team.

Want to see it for your team? DM me 'DEMO' 📲

posts.hook: Imagine this: a prospect tells your rep 'call me next week' 📞
posts.body:
Imagine this: a prospect tells your rep 'call me next week' 📞

Old system: rep writes it in his notebook. Life happens. The call never comes.

leads2conv system: a follow-up reminder is set right there. On the day, the rep gets notified. The lead gets called.

Deals don't die because people are bad salespeople.
They die because follow-ups get forgotten.

Automatic reminders mean no lead falls through the cracks — even when things get busy.

📲 DM us 'DEMO' to see how it works.

posts.body: "Call me next week." ⏰ With leads2conv, that reminder fires automatically. No forgotten follow-ups.

posts.hook: The loudest rep in the room is not always the best rep.
posts.body:
The loudest rep in the room is not always the best rep.

Every sales manager knows this — but without data, you end up rewarding confidence instead of performance.

leads2conv's team leaderboard changes that.

Calls made. Leads converted. Follow-ups completed.
Every rep ranked on the same scoreboard.

Quiet Ama who works methodically and closes consistently?
She finally gets seen.

Loud Kweku who talks a big game but skips follow-ups?
The numbers don't lie.

Fair teams run on data, not volume.

DM 'DEMO' and I'll show you what this looks like in practice. 📲

Full history. Full context. Zero interruption.

The business owns the pipeline. Always.

weeks.theme: COST + OBJECTIONS — ROI math in GHS, before/after, objection-busting, H2 urgency

posts.hook: Let's do the math on one forgotten follow-up.
posts.body:
Let's do the math on one forgotten follow-up.

Say your rep closes deals averaging GHS 3,000.
He forgets to call back a warm lead. That lead goes cold.
One lost deal: GHS 3,000 gone.

Now multiply that by 4 reps.
And by how many forgotten follow-ups happen every month.

leads2conv's automatic reminders exist for exactly this reason.
Every "call me next week" gets a follow-up. Automatically.

The cost of one recovered deal is almost certainly more than the cost of the tool itself.

H2 started this month. The pipeline you protect now is the commission you pay in September.

DM 'DEMO' — let's show you the numbers for your team. 📲
posts.graphic_brief: Simple calculation graphic: GHS 3,000 deal x 4 reps x forgotten follow-ups = big number in red. leads2conv logo.

posts.body: One forgotten follow-up = one lost deal. 💸 At GHS 3,000 a deal, that maths hurts.

posts.hook: "My team is small. I don't need software." Let me challenge that gently. 🙂
posts.body:
"My team is small. I don't need software." Let me challenge that gently. 🙂

Small teams lose leads too.
Small teams forget follow-ups too.
Small teams have reps who resign and take contacts too.

Actually, small teams feel the impact more — because every lost deal is a bigger percentage of your revenue.

leads2conv works for a team of 2 just as well as a team of 20.

Setup is fast. If your rep can use WhatsApp, they can use this.

Don't wait until the team is big enough to justify it. Start protecting your pipeline now.

DM us 'DEMO' 📲

posts.hook: Winners adjust in July, not January.
posts.body:
Winners adjust in July, not January.

H1 data is in. Or it should be.

If you can't look back at H1 with real numbers — actual calls made, actual leads worked, actual follow-ups completed — then July is a guessing game.

And H2 decisions made from guesses tend to produce the same H2 results as H1.

The businesses that will finish 2026 strong are adjusting their systems right now.
Not their vibes. Not their goals. Their systems.

leads2conv is one of those system adjustments.
Small. Fast to set up. Built here in Ghana.

Don't wait for December to wish you'd started in July.

DM 'DEMO' and let's talk this week. 📲

posts.body: Winners adjust in July, not January. 🏆 What system are you fixing this week?

posts.hook: Saturday thought for every sales manager in Ghana 🇬🇭
posts.body:
Saturday thought for every sales manager in Ghana 🇬🇭

The commission dispute at month-end?
It starts with no records in week one.

When nobody has a log of what happened, every rep remembers what's best for them.
"I made that referral." "That was my lead." "I followed up three times."

leads2conv keeps the record. For everyone.
No disputes. No awkward conversations. Just data.

Enjoy the weekend — but if this is a headache you recognize, we should talk next week. 😄

DM us 'DEMO' 📲

notes_for_founder: Confirm and fill in ⭐[OFFER] details by Friday 24 July so Week 4 posts can be finalised — all Week 4 graphics and copy depend on the specific offer (price, discount, bundle, or free trial terms).
All demo requests should be routed to WhatsApp +233 553528402 — ensure someone is monitoring this number daily from 20 July onwards as Week 4 will drive direct inbound traffic.

qa_report.issue: Hook 'Rep leaves.' too similar to 2026-07-03 whatsapp_status hook 'Rep leaves Friday.' — freshness risk within calendar. Rewritten to 'Pipeline stays put.' with new body.
'9 days left in July' is arithmetically wrong: July 23 to July 31 = 8 days remaining. Corrected to '8 days left'.
'9 days left on the July offer' is wrong — should be 8 days from July 23 to July 31. Corrected.
'closes on Thursday 31st' — July 31 2026 is a Friday per calendar logic (July 1 = Wednesday). Corrected to 'Friday 31st'.
'August 1st is 7 days away' — July 24 to August 1 = 8 days. Corrected to '8 days away'.
'closes Thursday 31st' — July 31 is a Friday. Corrected to 'Friday 31st'.
'Offer closes Thursday' — July 31 is a Friday. Corrected to 'Friday 31st'.
'closes this Thursday 31st July' — July 31 is a Friday. Corrected to 'Friday 31st July'.
Hook and body said 'closes Thursday' — July 31 is a Friday. Corrected to 'closes Friday' throughout.
'closes Thursday 31st' — July 31 is a Friday. Corrected to 'closes Friday 31st'.
'July offer ends Thursday' — July 31 is a Friday, not Thursday. Corrected to 'ends Friday'.

Hook 'Tomorrow is the last day' and body 'Tomorrow is decision day' imply July 30 is the last day, but offer closes July 31. Rewrote to 'Two days left. July offer closes Friday 31st.' Hook updated accordingly.
'Last day tomorrow' implies July 30 is last day; offer closes July 31. Corrected hook to '2 days left.' and body to reference Friday 31st.
Hook 'Today is the last day' and body 'July 31st. That's today' — post is dated July 30, not July 31. Rewritten as 'One day left. Offer closes tomorrow — Friday 31st July.'
'TODAY is the last day. July offer closes tonight' — post is dated July 30; offer closes July 31. Rewritten to 'Last chance — tomorrow is the deadline' with body referencing Friday 31st.
